is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of P. falciparum isolates and cytb gene polymorphism (title and throughout).

reports_target_markers.evidence: Pfcytb codon 268 examined (Y268S) by RFLP in all 477 isolates and sequencing of 270 isolates.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: No cytb mutation (0/477) detected in pre-treatment isolates; one Y268S in post-treatment isolate. Frequency of Y268S = 0/477 in field isolates is extractable.

sub_country_location.evidence: Frequencies tied only to country of infection (Cameroon, Ivory Coast, Mali, Senegal, Comoro Islands, Madagascar). Samples were collected in France from returning travellers; no sub-national location within the African source countries.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Samples collected between 1999 and 2004, a 6-year span, with cytb genotyping (0/477 mutations) pooled across the entire period, exceeding 3 years.

african_field_samples.evidence: Samples are imported/traveller cases collected in French hospitals from patients returning from Africa and the Indian Ocean, not African field/clinical isolates collected in situ.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study collecting isolates and performing in vitro susceptibility and genotyping.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples collected through the French National Reference Centre surveillance; routine field collection, no explicit statement of overlap with a specific larger published dataset.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary tables referenced for finer spatial or temporal resolution; data are in main text and Table 1.

assessment.summary: Study of atovaquone susceptibility and cytb (codon 268) polymorphism in 477 P. falciparum isolates from French travellers returning from Africa and the Indian Ocean (1999-2004). No Y268S mutation found in pre-treatment isolates. However, the samples are imported traveller cases (not African field isolates), frequencies are only resolvable at country level, and the 0/477 genotyping frequency pools samples across a 6-year span.
exclusion_reasons: Imported/traveller cases collected in France, not African field/clinical isolates
Frequencies resolvable only at country level, no sub-national resolution
cytb frequency pooled across 1999-2004 (6 years), exceeding 3-year temporal resolution
